what_it_does: This file is a ledger batch result that records a jury confirmation pass over 89 candidate UI elements drawn from the IA-mobile.html mockup, grouping them into 54 cluster dossiers and labeling each as confirmed or flagged with vote counts, grounding status, capability tags, and how-to-change annotations.
observations: There are 89 candidates, 89 dossiers, and 54 clusters; the file emits 28 confirmed and 26 flagged cluster dossiers whose cluster_members sum to 89.
Each dossier uses a uri-like address (ui://<region>/<name>), capability tags (pointable, presentable, spotlit, driven), a grounding label (built or proposed), and a howto block.
Confirmed dossiers have empty reasons arrays; flagged dossiers carry a templated Layer 2 SOFT reason about vote quorum.
Several addresses appear in both confirmed and flagged lists with different descriptions (e.g., ui://board/process, ui://board/utterance, ui://board/memo-gate).
Many howto blocks claim specific source files (Toolbar.tsx, Board.tsx, Chrome.tsx, runtime/suite.py) even when grounding is marked proposed or mockup-only.
The bottom tabbar (ui://chrome/tabbar) and primary RUN-run trigger (ui://chrome/run) are confirmed, anchoring the mobile navigation and action model.
Flagged entries include some large clusters (process cluster_size 9, status cluster_size 4, settings/panels cluster_size 4) that failed the grounding quorum.
standouts: A three-vote accuracy jury with explicit votes_grounded, votes_ungrounded, unanimous, and quorum fields decides each dossier's status.
The ledger distinguishes 'built' UI elements with claimed code paths from 'proposed' mockup-only elements.
Duplicate addresses coexist with opposite verdicts, suggesting unresolved naming collisions or overlapping concepts.
The RUN-run, NODE-portal, VOICE, ENG-resolve, RHM-decide, and SM-revert feature mappings tie UI gestures to backend wires.
conventions: Uses uri-like addresses of the form ui://<region>/<element>.
Consistent dossier schema: address, capabilities, grounding, howto, maps_to_feature, represents.
Cluster metadata (cluster_size, cluster_members) references candidate indices 0-88.
Jury results follow a fixed verdict structure with n=3, quorum threshold, and unanimous flag.
Breaks its own naming convention by allowing the same address to represent different semantics in confirmed and flagged sections.
concerns: Duplicate addresses with different meanings in confirmed and flagged sections could confuse downstream consumers.
Many 'proposed' items are confirmed despite howto stating 'mockup-only; no built code path maps to this yet', blurring the line between mockup presence and implementation grounding.
Confirmed entries provide no reasons, so the basis for acceptance is not inspectable.
Flagged reasons are repetitive templates and do not explain what specifically was ungrounded.
n_candidates equals n_dossiers (89) but only 54 dossier records are emitted, making the dossier count field ambiguous.
Source-file references in howto blocks are asserted without evidence in this file.
notes: A vote-backed audit trail that tries to separate real mockup elements from speculative ones, but lets several ambiguous concepts appear under the same address in both confirmed and flagged lists.
questions: What is the exact relationship between a candidate, a dossier, and a cluster when n_candidates equals n_dossiers but only 54 dossier records are present?
How are the three jury draws performed and what criteria make a vote 'grounded' versus 'ungrounded'?
Why are duplicate addresses allowed with opposite verdicts, and how should a consumer resolve them?
Are the source-file references in howto derived from the mockup, from actual code, or inferred by the jury?
What is the intended downstream action for confirmed versus flagged entries?
purpose_vs_actual: The ground text describes a warm, gold-themed mobile Board UI prototype for managing ideas and tasks; the JSON itself is not the prototype but a structured jury ledger that confirms or flags which candidate UI elements are considered grounded, so it audits the prototype rather than rendering it.
apparent_themes: Built versus proposed UI element distinction
intention_signals: It appears intended to create an auditable, human-validated bridge from a UI mockup to implementable feature codes and source regions, filtering out uncertain or hallucinated elements before downstream build steps.
novelty: The use of a three-vote accuracy jury with explicit quorum/unanimity flags and templated 'variance not error' reasoning, plus the coexistence of duplicate addresses with opposite verdicts.
contribution: It contributes a structured, vote-annotated inventory of UI element candidates with links to feature codes and source regions, which a fused system could keep as a traceability and audit layer between the mobile mockup and implementation intent.
summary_for_embedding: rg10-batch-IA-mobile.json is a 54-cluster jury ledger confirming or flagging 89 candidate UI elements from the IA-mobile.html mobile Board mockup, annotated with addresses, capabilities, grounding status, feature mappings, and vote counts.
intention_for_embedding: The file was produced to audit and validate which UI elements in the mobile mockup are grounded enough to be treated as actionable design-to-code substrates.
suggested_fields: cluster_label or dossier_label to disambiguate duplicate addresses
mockup_selector or source_region_bbox to anchor each element visually
downstream_action indicating what happens to confirmed versus flagged entries
proposed_edge_kinds: grounds (mockup element to claimed code region)
conflicts-with (between duplicate address dossiers with opposite verdicts)
